title: Pleurocoronis gentryi
taxonomy: Pleurocoronis gentryi is classified within the family Asteraceae, commonly known as the daisy or sunflower family. This classification places it within the order Asterales and the clade Eudicots. Further, Pleurocoronis gentryi belongs to the clade Angiosperms, which includes flowering plants. It is native to Mexico and is part of the genus Pleurocoronis, containing only one species. The exact classification of this plant is further detailed within the broader plant kingdom, specifically within the kingdom Plantae.
uses: Pleurocoronis gentryi is a plant species native to Mexico, and it has been traditionally used in folk medicine. intro: Pleurocoronis gentryi, also known as Gentry's silverlace, is a perennial plant species native to the southwestern United States and northern Mexico. This plant has been traditionally used in herbal medicine to treat various ailments, with potential health benefits including anti-inflammatory, antimicrobial, and antispasmodic properties. The bioactive constituents of Pleurocoronis gentryi include sesquiterpene lactones, flavonoids, and phenolic acids, which are responsible for its medicinal properties.
